items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 4:55AM CDT by NWS La Crosse WI
items.summary:
The combination of strong south winds with dry air and parched fuels
results in elevated fire weather conditions in west-central and
central Wisconsin this afternoon and early evening, primarily near
and north of Interstate 90.

Minimum relative humidity values of 15 to 25 percent expected with
sustained winds of 15 to 25 mph gusting to 35 mph.

Under these conditions, fires will start and spread more easily.
Please exercise caution with any activities that involve outdoor
fires or create sparks
items.area: Taylor; Clark; Buffalo; Trempealeau; Jackson; La Crosse; Monroe; Juneau; Adams

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 4:13AM CDT by NWS Duluth MN
items.summary:
Dry conditions and breezy winds will lead to near critical fire
weather conditions Saturday. Northwest winds of 15 to 20 mph with
gusts to 35 mph are forecast for Saturday afternoon. Minimum
relative humidity values of 15 to 25 percent are also expected.
Together, these conditions could lead to the rapid spread of
fires. Check burning restrictions and fire danger before burning.

For more information on burning restrictions for Wisconsin, see
apps.dnr.wi.gov/wisburn.
items.area: Burnett; Washburn

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 5:11AM EDT by NWS Baltimore MD/Washington DC
items.summary:
The combination of drying fine fuels despite recent light rainfall,
relative humidities around 15 to 25 percent, and northwest wind
gusts around 20 to 30 mph will lead to an increased risk for the
rapid spread of wildfires today. The poorest conditions are
expected between about 10 AM this morning and 4 PM this afternoon.

Residents and visitors are urged to properly dispose of cigarette
butts, matches, and any other flammable items in appropriate
containers. Additionally, exercise caution if op

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 4:49AM EDT by NWS Jacksonville FL
items.summary:
Areas of locally dense fog have formed during the predawn hours
across the Suwannee Valley and north central Florida, with
visibilities falling below one-half mile locally. Visibilities
will improve before the mid morning hours today.

Motorists early this morning should be alert for sudden changes
in visibility. When encountering fog, slow down, increase
following distance, and use low-beam headlights.
items.area: Suwannee; Union; Bradford; Gilchrist; Eastern Alachua; Western Alachua; Central Marion; Western Marion; Southeastern Columbia; Southwestern Columbia

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 4:48AM EDT by NWS Mount Holly NJ
items.summary:
There is an elevated risk for fire spread today for Delaware and
the Eastern Shore of Maryland. Minimum relative humidity values
will fall into the 20-30% range. This will be accompanied by
northwest winds around 15 to 25 MPH and gusts up to 35 MPH. These
conditions, along with the continued drying of fine fuels, could
support the rapid spread of any fires that ignite, which could
quickly become difficult to control. Conditions improve close to
sunset as winds decrease and RHs increase.

Outdoor
items.area: New Castle; Kent; Inland Sussex; Delaware Beaches; Kent; Queen Anne's; Talbot; Caroline

items.summary:
* WHAT...Dangerous rip currents.

* WHERE...Gulf County Beaches.

* WHEN...Through late Sunday night.

* IMPACTS...Rip currents can sweep even the best swimmers away
from shore into deeper water.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...The rip current risk will be high along
west-facing beaches today, then shifting to the south-facing
beaches on Sunday.

occurred Friday night.

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 2:20AM EDT by NWS Newport/Morehead City NC
items.summary:
The combination of breezy northerly winds gusting as high as 25 to
30 mph, ongoing drought, and relative humidity values in the 25
to 30 percent range will lead to an increased fire danger for
portions of Eastern North Carolina today.

Outdoor burning is discouraged. Please refer to your local burn
permitting authority on whether you can burn. If you do burn, use
extreme caution and ensure fire suppression equipment is readily
available.
items.area: Pitt; Greene; Beaufort; Duplin; Lenoir; Jones; Pamlico; Northern Craven; Southern Craven; West Carteret; Inland Onslow; Coastal Onslow

possible.

* WHERE...Tidal Potomac River from Key Bridge to Smith Point VA,
and Chesapeake Bay and adjoining estuaries north of Smith
Point VA.

* WHEN...From 4 AM to 8 AM EDT this morning.

* IMPACTS...Strong winds may cause hazardous waves which could
capsize or damage vessels.
